target.title: fix: prevent bundleMcpRuntime/bundleLspRuntime variable shadowing in embedded attempt
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #87482 — MCP filesystem sidecar duplicate spawn / process leak caused by variable shadowing.

## Root Cause

In `runEmbeddedAttempt()` (`src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts`):

- **Line 846**: `let bundleMcpRuntime` declared at function scope for cleanup tracking
- **Line 1364**: `const bundleMcpRuntime` declared in a nested block scope, **shadowing** the outer `let`
- **Line 4747**: cleanup references the outer `let`, which is still `undefined`

Same pattern for `bundleLspRuntime` (lines 847, 1378, 4748).

When cleanup ran `cleanupEmbeddedAttemptResources()`, it passed `undefined` for both runtimes, so `dispose()` was never called. This meant:
1. MCP leases were never released (`activeLeases > 0` persisted)
2. The session runtime manager skipped idle eviction
3. Stdio MCP child processes accumulated indefinitely

## Fix

Removed `const` from the inner declarations (lines 1364 and 1378) so they assign to the outer `let` variables instead of creating shadowing block-scoped bindings.

**Files changed:**
- `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts` — 2 lines changed
- `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.subscription-cleanup.test.ts` — 2 regression tests added

## Tests

- Added test verifying `bundleMcpRuntime` and `bundleLspRuntime` are properly disposed during cleanup
- Added test verifying graceful handling when both are `undefined`
- All 12 existing + new tests pass
target.author: hoobnn

commands.author: luoyanglang

target.title: fix(update): preserve newer managed plugin pins
target.body:
`openclaw update` could silently downgrade a user-pinned managed plugin direct dependency, reintroducing plugin hotfix bugs and leaving the managed npm root inconsistent with user intent.

Fixes #85184.

## Affected surface

- `src/plugins/install.ts`
  - `installPluginFromManagedNpmRoot`
  - managed npm update-mode resolution handling
- `src/infra/npm-managed-root.ts`
  - `upsertManagedNpmRootDependency`
  - managed root direct dependency spec reading
- Tests:
  - `src/plugins/install.npm-spec.test.ts`
  - `src/infra/npm-managed-root.test.ts`

## Scope

This is a narrow update-path guard for exact semver direct dependency pins. It does not introduce a new config flag and does not change general install-mode behavior.

- In `mode: "update"`, detect when the managed npm root already has a newer exact direct dependency spec than the update target.
- If that newer version is already installed with lockfile metadata, reuse the installed package instead of running `npm install` and downgrading it.
- If the package is pinned newer and installed but lockfile metadata is missing, re-resolve npm metadata for the preserved newer spec before install/verification so integrity and shasum describe the same version.
- If preserved-version npm metadata cannot be resolved, fail closed instead of synthesizing a version-only registry resolution.
- Non-semver specs, equal versions, older pins, manual install mode, and unrelated dependency updates keep the existing behavior.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: After this patch, an `openclaw update`-mode plugin install preserves a user-pinned newer exact managed npm root dependency instead of downgrading it to the older bundled update target. When matching lockfile metadata is missing, the update path re-resolves metadata for the preserved newer version so `version`, `integrity`, and lockfile state stay consistent.
- Real environment tested: Linux local development executor, current PR head `b89d779cc671`, real OpenClaw source imported with `node --import tsx`, real managed npm root mutation through `installPluginFromNpmSpec(..., { mode: "update" })`, real npm registry package `@martian-engineering/lossless-claw`, temporary proof directory outside the repository with repository path redacted as `<repo>`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Create a temporary managed npm root outside the repository; install/pin `@martian-engineering/lossless-claw@0.11.2`; request update-mode install for older bundled target `@martian-engineering/lossless-claw@0.10.0`; verify manifest, installed package, and lockfile remain on `0.11.2`; repeat after removing matching lockfile metadata to verify metadata is re-resolved for `0.11.2`.
- Evidence after fix: Terminal transcript from the mutating managed npm root proof command:

```text
OPENCLAW_PROOF_HEAD=b89d779cc671 node --import tsx /tmp/openclaw-pr87304-proof.mjs
```

Redacted proof setup output:

```json
{
  "proof": "OpenClaw PR #87304 managed npm root update proof",
  "head": "b89d779cc671",
  "repository": "<repo>",
  "packageName": "@martian-engineering/lossless-claw",
  "olderSpec": "@martian-engineering/lossless-claw@0.10.0",
  "newerSpec": "@martian-engineering/lossless-claw@0.11.2"
}
```

Scenario 1: existing newer install with lockfile metadata. The update request was for `0.10.0`, but the managed root kept the user-pinned `0.11.2`.

```json
{
  "scenario": "existing-installed-and-locked",
  "input": {
    "requestedUpdateSpec": "@martian-engineering/lossless-claw@0.10.0",
    "existingPackageJsonPin": "0.11.2",
    "existingInstalledVersion": "0.11.2",
    "lockfilePresentBeforeUpdate": true
  },
  "observedResult": {
    "ok": true,
    "resultVersion": "0.11.2",
    "resultResolutionVersion": "0.11.2",
    "resultResolutionIntegrity": "sha512-5jr7KIMeXY1FTACoGOVrxXPCdX87ZVrURZJa51HGmezrQ77UaOG6BFb5E3A6sRE+KliJGnkCdpam2KICRZrkSA==",
    "finalPackageJsonPin": "0.11.2",
    "finalInstalledVersion": "0.11.2",
    "finalLockVersion": "0.11.2",
    "finalLockIntegrity": "sha512-5jr7KIMeXY1FTACoGOVrxXPCdX87ZVrURZJa51HGmezrQ77UaOG6BFb5E3A6sRE+KliJGnkCdpam2KICRZrkSA=="
  }
}
```

Scenario 2: existing newer install but missing lockfile metadata. The update path re-resolved the preserved `0.11.2` metadata, rebuilt lockfile metadata, and still did not downgrade to `0.10.0`.

```json
{
  "scenario": "existing-installed-lockfile-missing",
  "input": {
    "requestedUpdateSpec": "@martian-engineering/lossless-claw@0.10.0",
    "existingPackageJsonPin": "0.11.2",
    "existingInstalledVersion": "0.11.2",
    "lockfilePresentBeforeUpdate": false
  },
  "observedResult": {
    "ok": true,
    "resultVersion": "0.11.2",
    "resultResolutionVersion": "0.11.2",
    "resultResolutionIntegrity": "sha512-5jr7KIMeXY1FTACoGOVrxXPCdX87ZVrURZJa51HGmezrQ77UaOG6BFb5E3A6sRE+KliJGnkCdpam2KICRZrkSA==",
    "finalPackageJsonPin": "0.11.2",
    "finalInstalledVersion": "0.11.2",
    "finalLockVersion": "0.11.2",
    "finalLockIntegrity": "sha512-5jr7KIMeXY1FTACoGOVrxXPCdX87ZVrURZJa51HGmezrQ77UaOG6BFb5E3A6sRE+KliJGnkCdpam2KICRZrkSA=="
  }
}
```

Before-fix RED evidence from the source regression:

```text
FAIL |plugins| src/plugins/install.npm-spec.test.ts > installPluginFromNpmSpec > reuses a newer installed managed dependency during update instead of downgrading
Error: npm install resolved @martian-engineering/lossless-claw to version 0.10.0, expected 0.11.2
```

ClawSweeper P2 repair evidence: head `b89d779cc671` also adds a fail-closed regression for preserved newer pins when the preserved-version metadata lookup fails. The update now returns an error before `npm install` instead of proceeding with a synthetic version-only resolution.

Local validation after the patch:

```text
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.infra.config.ts src/infra/npm-managed-root.test.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  19 passed (19)

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.plugins.config.ts src/plugins/install.npm-spec.test.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  41 passed (41)

$ pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/plugins/install.ts src/plugins/install.npm-spec.test.ts src/infra/npm-managed-root.ts src/infra/npm-managed-root.test.ts
All matched files use the correct format.

$ pnpm lint --threads=8
[oxlint:core] finished
[oxlint:extensions] finished
[oxlint:scripts] finished
(exit 0)

$ node scripts/check-changed.mjs --base origin/main
[check:changed] lanes=core, coreTests
Import cycle check: 0 runtime value cycle(s).
(exit 0)

$ git diff --check origin/main..HEAD && git diff --check
(exit 0)

$ node scripts/check-no-conflict-markers.mjs
(exit 0)

$ gitleaks protect --staged --redact
no leaks found
```

- Observed result after fix: Update-mode install did not downgrade the managed root dependency from `0.11.2` to `0.10.0`. The returned plugin install result, final `package.json` pin, installed package version, `package-lock.json` version, and lockfile integrity all stayed on `0.11.2`. The missing-lockfile-metadata path regenerated metadata for `0.11.2`, avoiding stale integrity. The preserved-metadata failure regression now fails before `npm install`, so the preserved path no longer proceeds with a version-only synthetic registry resolution.
- What was not tested: I did not run a full package-manager `openclaw update --tag` against a real user home directory. I did not run the proof against a live Gateway process; this patch is limited to the managed npm root plugin update/install boundary.

## Coverage note

I re-ran the required no-collision searches immediately before pushing this branch. The only returned PR is this PR, `#87304`; no competing open PR covers `#85184`:

```text
gh search prs --repo openclaw/openclaw --state open "Fixes #85184"
gh search prs --repo openclaw/openclaw --state open "#85184"
gh search prs --repo openclaw/openclaw --state open "upsertManagedNpmRootDependency"
gh search prs --repo openclaw/openclaw --state open "npm-managed-root.ts downgrade"
gh search prs --repo openclaw/openclaw --state open "openclaw update pinned dependency"
```

Recent related work in this area covers managed peer dependencies and bundled plugin install planning, but it does not cover direct dependency downgrade prevention for an already-pinned managed npm root package.
target.author: luoyanglang
